name: Cauliflower and Roquefort Soup
recipeIngredient: A few drops of liquid pepper for seasoning
recipeInstructions:
Melt butter in a large, heavy Dutch oven over moderately high heat. Saute onion for 1 minute, then cover and sweat for 10 minutes. Halve and core the cauliflower, reserving some florets. Chop the remaining cauliflower. Peel and dice the potato. Add the chopped cauliflower and diced potato to the Dutch oven; cook, stirring, for 1 minute. Add chicken broth, chives, Herbes de Provence, and liquid pepper seasoning. Bring to a boil, then cover and lower the heat. Simmer for 10 minutes, or until the vegetables are just tender. Cool slightly. Puree all but 1 1/2 cups of the mixture in batches using a food processor or blender, returning it to the pan. In a small bowl, blend heavy cream, egg yolks, and Armagnac. Add 1 cup of the hot soup to the bowl, beating constantly with a wire whisk. Return to the pan and add half of the crumbled Roquefort. Heat very slowly, stirring constantly, until the cheese melts and the soup thickens slightly. Do not allow the soup to boil. Garnish the soup with reserved cauliflower florets, crumbled Roquefort, and chives.
Makes 4 to 6 servings.
